channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The best new Windows 11 features of 2024
item.description: Microsoft is a little sneaky. Sure, there’s just one “big” update for Windows 11 each year. But Microsoft’s Windows team is always working on something, and new Windows 11 features are arriving on PCs every month — even outside of those high-profile updates.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI ready to hit its stride in schools in 2025
item.description: Experts predict that 2025 will be the year artificial intelligence (AI) truly gets off the ground in K-12 schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: 7 azioni chiave da intraprendere nel 2025 per un CIO sempre più autorevole
item.description: Nei prossimi 12 mesi i CIO hanno due grandi priorità: sviluppare un solido business case per la spesa in infrastruttura per l’intelligenza artificiale e aumentare la cybersicurezza e la resilienza.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk throws support behind Johnson for Speaker
item.description: Elon Musk backed Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, as the top Republican in the House attempts to secure support ahead of Friday’s Speaker vote. The tech mogul’s vote of confidence followed President-elect Trump’s “Complete & Total” endorsement of Johnson earlier in the day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Change management: Achieve your goals with the right change model
item.description: Change and transformation. Everyone talks about it and knows how necessary it is to adapt quickly to permanently changing conditions. And yet change is difficult.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The iPhone SE 4 price leaks again – and it could cost more than the iPhone SE 3
item.description: It seems as though Apple is aiming to get the price of the iPhone SE 4 under $500, based on the latest rumor.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 5 reasons why I’m finally upgrading to Windows 11 in January
item.description: Been putting off that Windows 11 upgrade? Here’s why the time might finally be right to take the plunge.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump sides with Musk on H-1B visas
item.description: President-elect Trump backed nonimmigrant visas for highly skilled workers as the program has been in the spotlight after Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy faced backlash within the Republican leader's base for signaling their support for the H-1B work visa, which has been criticized as too complicated
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Tech investor Cathie Wood on how Trump can boost the U.S. economy
item.description: U.S. tech investor Cathie Wood is calling on Donald Trump’s incoming administration to boost economic growth and policy certainty by backdating promised corporate and personal tax cuts to Jan. 1, 2025, she told Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has asked the Supreme Court to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors
item.description: Learn how rich text editors help government agencies ensure compliance with data security, accessibility, and document standardization. The post Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in the president-elect’s political movement into public display.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber
item.description: From Volt Typhoon to Salt Typhoon, major cyber incidents in 2024 shined a spotlight on how agencies are managing cyber threats to critical infrastructure. The post 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
